title: The amenity basket
text: Read six category ids free catalog, turn city name centre point.
code: library(closecity) library(sf) close <- close_client("ck_live_your_key")   # use your own key here types <- close$destination_types() ids <- setNames(types$dest_type_id, types$label)  basket <- c(   grocery = ids[["grocery_stores"]],   library = ids[["libraries"]],   park = ids[["parks"]],   transit = ids[["frequent_transit"]],   restaurant = ids[["restaurants"]],   cafe = ids[["cafes"]] )  city <- close$places("Richmond")[1, ]

title: The amenity basket
text: $records() reads every page block query: walk time every block near downtown six categories, along block’s population. result one sf table.
code: blocks <- close$records(   "blocks_query",   center = list(lon = city$lon, lat = city$lat), radius_m = 2500,   mode = "walk", type = unname(basket), include_population = TRUE )  # One row per block, for population and for mapping. one_per_block <- blocks[!duplicated(blocks$geoid), ] total_pop <- sum(one_per_block$population)

what: Coverage, one amenity at a time
title: The amenity basket
text: amenity, block counts covered within 15-minute walk. Add population covered blocks. Parks restaurants tend everywhere; groceries frequent transit usually hardest reach. Map one amenity see pattern.
code: for (name in names(basket)) {   covered <- unique(blocks$geoid[blocks$dest_type_id == basket[name] &                                    blocks$travel_time <= 15])   pop <- sum(one_per_block$population[one_per_block$geoid %in% covered])   cat(sprintf("%-11s %3.0f%%\n", name, 100 * pop / total_pop)) } #> grocery      13% #> library      38% #> park         89% #> transit       5% #> restaurant   71% #> cafe         64% near_transit <- unique(blocks$geoid[blocks$dest_type_id == basket["transit"] &                                       blocks$travel_time <= 15]) one_per_block$has_transit <- one_per_block$geoid %in% near_transit  plot(one_per_block["has_transit"], pal = c("#eef0f7", "#058040"), border = NA)

title: The amenity basket
text: Count, block, many six amenities within 15-minute walk. score, 0 6, map planners reach . reuses data already pulled, costs nothing .
code: covered <- blocks[blocks$travel_time <= 15, ] score <- tapply(covered$dest_type_id, covered$geoid, function(x) length(unique(x))) one_per_block$score <- as.integer(score[one_per_block$geoid]) one_per_block$score[is.na(one_per_block$score)] <- 0L  plot(one_per_block["score"], pal = hcl.colors(7, "viridis"), border = NA)

title: The amenity basket
text: block fully covered six amenities within 15 minutes. Start every block remove ones miss amenity.
code: covered_all <- one_per_block$geoid for (name in names(basket)) {   covered <- unique(blocks$geoid[blocks$dest_type_id == basket[name] &                                    blocks$travel_time <= 15])   covered_all <- intersect(covered_all, covered) }  basket_pop <- sum(one_per_block$population[one_per_block$geoid %in% covered_all]) cat(sprintf("All six amenities: %.0f%% of residents\n", 100 * basket_pop / total_pop)) #> All six amenities: 4% of residents  one_per_block$full_basket <- one_per_block$geoid %in% covered_all plot(one_per_block["full_basket"], pal = c("#eef0f7", "#f36e21"), border = NA)

title: The amenity basket
text: Look residents yet fully covered, count many missing amenity. amenity people lack one add first. Map uncovered blocks see new amenities good.
code: uncovered <- setdiff(one_per_block$geoid, covered_all)  for (name in names(basket)) {   covered <- unique(blocks$geoid[blocks$dest_type_id == basket[name] &                                    blocks$travel_time <= 15])   lacking <- setdiff(uncovered, covered)   pop <- sum(one_per_block$population[one_per_block$geoid %in% lacking])   cat(sprintf("%-11s %6.0f residents would gain access\n", name, pop)) } #> grocery      26219 residents would gain access #> library      18720 residents would gain access #> park          3373 residents would gain access #> transit      28424 residents would gain access #> restaurant    8732 residents would gain access #> cafe         10798 residents would gain access one_per_block$uncovered <- one_per_block$geoid %in% uncovered plot(one_per_block["uncovered"], pal = c("#eef0f7", "#202a5b"), border = NA)

title: Get started with closecity
text: terms come throughout: Census block. smallest area Census Bureau publishes. one 15-digit id, GEOID. Destination type. category place, grocery stores libraries. Every type numeric id. Mode. someone travels: walk, bike, transit. Isochrone. area reachable point within time limit, polygon. Catchment. reverse: every block can reach given place.

title: Get started with closecity
text: make every request client object. catalog lookup routes free, close_client() key also works .
code: library(closecity) close <- close_client("ck_live_your_key")   # use your own key here close$modes() #>   mode_id    mode    description #> 1       1    walk        Walking #> 2       2    bike         Biking #> 3       3 transit Public transit

what: Look things up instead of guessing
title: Get started with closecity
text: Two free calls save memorising codes. come back data frames, filter index usual way: read numeric id category catalog, turn city name GEOID centre point.
code: types <- close$destination_types() grocery_id <- types$dest_type_id[types$label == "grocery_stores"]  providence <- close$places("Providence")[1, ] providence$geoid #> [1] "4459000"

what: Make a call and map it
title: Get started with closecity
text: Routes geometry return sf object, can map result straight away.
code: groceries <- close$pois_search(lat = providence$lat, lon = providence$lon,                                radius_m = 1500, type = grocery_id) plot(st_geometry(groceries), pch = 19, col = "#202a5b")

title: Get started with closecity
text: Every route returns tabular data default. output setting controls shape: "spatial" (default) gives sf object geometry applies data frame otherwise; "tabular" gives data frame everywhere never downloads block boundaries; "raw" gives underlying reply. Set client, pass output = single call.

title: Get started with closecity
text: Failed requests raise classed condition. Catch base close_api_error, specific one.
code: tryCatch(   close$block_summary("000000000000000"),   close_api_error = function(e) message(sprintf("%s (%d)", e$slug, e$status)) ) #> block-not-found (404)

title: Competitor walksheds
text: Read cafe category id free catalog find city centre.
code: library(closecity) library(sf) close <- close_client("ck_live_your_key")   # use your own key here types <- close$destination_types() ids <- setNames(types$dest_type_id, types$label) cafe <- ids[["cafes"]]  city <- close$places("Providence")[1, ]

title: Competitor walksheds
text: Search cafes near downtown. result sf points, plot pick one shop subject.
code: cafes <- close$pois_search(lat = city$lat, lon = city$lon,                            radius_m = 1200, type = cafe) plot(st_geometry(cafes), pch = 19, col = "#202a5b") ours <- cafes[1, ] ours$name #> [1] "The Nitro Bar"

title: Competitor walksheds
text: Ask every block can reach shop within 10-minute walk. comes back sf polygons, block boundaries downloaded tigris.
code: our_shed <- close$poi_catchment(ours$dest_id, mode = "walk", max_minutes = 10)  plot(st_geometry(our_shed), col = "#eef0f7", border = "#c6cbe0") plot(st_geometry(ours), add = TRUE, pch = 19, col = "#f36e21", cex = 1.6)

title: Competitor walksheds
text: nearest competitors, pull walkshed count blocks share . our_shed$geoid just vector block ids, overlap plain set intersection.
code: for (i in 2:6) {   their_shed <- close$poi_catchment(cafes$dest_id[i], mode = "walk",                                     max_minutes = 10)   shared <- intersect(our_shed$geoid, their_shed$geoid)   cat(sprintf("%-28s %3d shared blocks (%.0f%% of ours)\n",               cafes$name[i], length(shared),               100 * length(shared) / nrow(our_shed))) } #> Schaste                       82 shared blocks (82% of ours) #> White Electric                53 shared blocks (53% of ours) #> Starbucks                     50 shared blocks (50% of ours) #> Little City Coffee & Kitchen  12 shared blocks (12% of ours) #> Cafe La France                 1 shared blocks (1% of ours)

title: Competitor walksheds
text: Draw walkshed, every cafe top, shop highlighted. clusters competitors inside walkshed ones competing walk-traffic.  recipe works wider area: search bounding box instead radius, loop shops.
code: plot(st_geometry(our_shed), col = "#eef0f7", border = "#c6cbe0") plot(st_geometry(cafes), add = TRUE, pch = 19, col = "#202a5b") plot(st_geometry(ours), add = TRUE, pch = 19, col = "#f36e21", cex = 1.6)

title: Your first walkability map
text: Build client, read need free catalog.
code: library(closecity) library(sf) close <- close_client("ck_live_your_key")   # use your own key here types <- close$destination_types() grocery <- types$dest_type_id[types$label == "grocery_stores"]  downtown <- close$places("Providence")[1, ]

title: Your first walkability map
text: Pick block ask long takes walk kind amenity. result small data frame, one row per category.

title: Your first walkability map
text: radius search returns points, ready map.
code: groceries <- close$pois_search(lat = downtown$lat, lon = downtown$lon,                                radius_m = 1200, type = grocery) plot(st_geometry(groceries), pch = 19, col = "#058040")

what: Draw how far you can walk
title: Your first walkability map
text: isochrone headline map: area can reach foot 10, 20, 30 minutes. contours come back largest first, drawing paints nearer times top.
code: rings <- close$isochrone(block = "440070008001068", mode = "walk",                          direction = "from", contours = c(10, 20, 30)) plot(rings["contour"], pal = hcl.colors(3, "YlGnBu", rev = TRUE),      border = "white", main = "Walk time from downtown (minutes)")

title: Your first walkability map
text: block, 30-minute budget, two modes side side. clearest way see bus buys .
code: walk <- close$isochrone(block = "440070008001068", mode = "walk",                         direction = "from", minutes = 30) transit <- close$isochrone(block = "440070008001068", mode = "transit",                            direction = "from", minutes = 30)  par(mfrow = c(1, 2)) plot(st_geometry(walk), col = "#05804033", border = "#058040",      main = "Walk, 30 min") plot(st_geometry(transit), col = "#202a5b33", border = "#202a5b",      main = "Transit, 30 min")

title: Your first walkability map
text: Looking home: find blocks near several amenities , narrow commute. amenity basket: population-weighted walkability coverage across whole city. Competitor walksheds: else draws neighbourhood.

title: Looking for a home
text: Build client, read pieces need free catalog instead memorising codes.
code: library(closecity) library(sf) close <- close_client("ck_live_your_key")   # use your own key here # The catalog lists every category with its numeric id. Pull the ids you need. types <- close$destination_types() ids <- setNames(types$dest_type_id, types$label)  grocery <- ids[["grocery_stores"]] restaurant <- ids[["restaurants"]] transit <- ids[["frequent_transit"]]  # Turn the city name into a GEOID and a centre point. city <- close$places("Somerville")[1, ]

title: Looking for a home
text: Look raw ingredients first. search returns points, plot three amenities three layers.
code: groceries <- close$pois_search(lat = city$lat, lon = city$lon,                                radius_m = 3000, type = grocery) restaurants <- close$pois_search(lat = city$lat, lon = city$lon,                                  radius_m = 3000, type = restaurant) stops <- close$pois_search(lat = city$lat, lon = city$lon,                            radius_m = 3000, type = transit)  plot(st_geometry(restaurants), col = "#c6cbe0", pch = 19, cex = 0.6) plot(st_geometry(groceries), col = "#058040", pch = 19, add = TRUE) plot(st_geometry(stops), col = "#f36e21", pch = 17, add = TRUE)

title: Looking for a home
text: Somerville census place, one call place GEOID pulls per-block walk times every block city. $records() reads every page returns one sf table one row per (block, category); block boundaries come tigris, downloaded cached. search arbitrary area instead, use $blocks_query() centre radius polygon. amenity rule, pick blocks pass one, keep blocks pass three.
code: blocks <- close$records("place_blocks", city$geoid, mode = "walk",                         type = c(grocery, restaurant, transit)) near_grocery <- unique(blocks$geoid[blocks$dest_type_id == grocery &                                       blocks$travel_time <= 10]) near_restaurant <- unique(blocks$geoid[blocks$dest_type_id == restaurant &                                          blocks$travel_time <= 5]) near_transit <- unique(blocks$geoid[blocks$dest_type_id == transit &                                       blocks$travel_time <= 20])  candidates <- intersect(intersect(near_grocery, near_restaurant), near_transit) winners <- blocks[blocks$geoid %in% candidates, ]  plot(st_geometry(blocks), col = "#eef0f7", border = "white") plot(st_geometry(winners), col = "#f36e21", border = NA, add = TRUE)

title: Looking for a home
text: Suppose two work different places. transit isochrone workplace shows far commute reaches. come back polygons.  Keep winning blocks also sit inside commutes. short list look.
code: work_a <- close$isochrone(lon = -71.0865, lat = 42.3625, mode = "transit",                           direction = "from", minutes = 20) work_b <- close$isochrone(lon = -71.0589, lat = 42.3555, mode = "transit",                           direction = "from", minutes = 20)  plot(st_geometry(work_a), col = "#05804033", border = "#058040") plot(st_geometry(work_b), col = "#f36e2133", border = "#f36e21", add = TRUE) both_commutes <- st_intersection(st_union(work_a), st_union(work_b)) shortlist <- winners[st_intersects(winners, both_commutes, sparse = FALSE)[, 1], ]  plot(st_geometry(winners), col = "#eef0f7", border = "white") plot(st_geometry(shortlist), col = "#058040", border = NA, add = TRUE)

title: Efficient token use
text: whole catalog free keyless: $modes(), $destination_types(), $vintage(), $places(), $isochrone_meta(), $last_updated(), $health(). lookups spend token. Revalidation free . Keep reply’s etag pass back if_none_match; unchanged response returns free 304. Read output = "raw" want etag:
code: close$output <- "raw" first <- close$block_summary("440070008001068", mode = "walk") again <- close$block_summary("440070008001068", mode = "walk",                              if_none_match = first$etag) again$not_modified   # TRUE, and nothing was charged #> [1] TRUE

title: Efficient token use
text: rows query returns multiply modes times types times blocks: Pass mode. Omitting returns three modes, three times rows. Request leaf types, parents. parent type expands leaves, single leaf id fraction rows parent like parks. Shrink area. Cost grows square radius_m, halving radius roughly quarter tokens. Use max_minutes. drops rows filter anyway.

what: Isochrones are the cheap reach primitive
title: Efficient token use
text: isochrone 10 tokens per contour regardless area, format = "blocks" returns every reachable block minutes. whole walkshed 10 tokens, catchment block query charges per block:
code: shed <- close$isochrone(block = "440070008001068", minutes = 30, mode = "walk",                         format = "blocks")

title: Efficient token use
text: Every metered reply carries token counts. frame output modes attached attributes; output = "raw" reply. want numbers, output = "tabular" skips block-boundary download entirely. block boundaries spatial mode joins cached locally, re-running script costs time, tokens; pass block_geometry frame already skip even .
code: close$output <- "tabular" groceries <- close$pois_search(lat = 41.823, lon = -71.412, radius_m = 1200) attr(groceries, "tokens_charged") #> [1] 100 attr(groceries, "tokens_remaining") #> [1] 999853046

title: Client for the Close API (api.close.city)
text: R client Close API. Get travel times every US census block nearby places, foot, bike, public transit. data behind close.city, read Close API. Documentation: https://henryspatialanalysis.github.io/closecity-r/

title: Client for the Close API (api.close.city)
code: # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("henryspatialanalysis/closecity-r")

title: Client for the Close API (api.close.city)
text: make requests client object. Feature results come back sf objects, can map right away.  Catalog lookup routes free need key:
code: library(closecity) close <- close_client("ck_live_your_key")   # use your own key here # Grocery stores within a 1.5 km walk of a point (type 30 is grocery stores): groceries <- close$pois_search(lat = 41.823, lon = -71.412, radius_m = 1500, type = 30) plot(sf::st_geometry(groceries), pch = 19, col = "#202a5b") close$modes()                    # walk, bike, transit #>   mode_id    mode    description #> 1       1    walk        Walking #> 2       2    bike         Biking #> 3       3 transit Public transit close$places("Providence")       # a city name to its GEOID and centre #> Simple feature collection with 9 features and 4 fields #> Geometry type: POINT #> Dimension:     XY #> Bounding box:  xmin: -111.8133 ymin: 32.34238 xmax: -71.35821 ymax: 42.28133 #> Geodetic CRS:  WGS 84 #>                 name   geoid        lon      lat                   geometry #> 1         Providence 4459000  -71.41872 41.82301 POINT (-71.41872 41.82301) #> 2         Providence 4962360 -111.81331 41.70341 POINT (-111.8133 41.70341) #> 3 Providence Village 4859748  -96.95432 33.23890  POINT (-96.95432 33.2389) #> 4         Providence 2163372  -87.75094 37.39935 POINT (-87.75094 37.39935) #> 5         Providence 0162688  -87.77611 32.34238 POINT (-87.77611 32.34238) #> 6    East Providence 4422960  -71.35821 41.80049 POINT (-71.35821 41.80049) #> 7     New Providence 3451810  -74.40343 40.69964 POINT (-74.40343 40.69964) #> 8    Lake Providence 2241400  -91.18247 32.81322 POINT (-91.18247 32.81322) #> 9     New Providence 1956415  -93.17162 42.28133 POINT (-93.17162 42.28133)

title: Client for the Close API (api.close.city)
text: Census block. smallest area Census Bureau publishes. one 15-digit id called GEOID. Destination type. category place, grocery stores libraries. type numeric id. Look close$destination_types(). Mode. someone travels: walk, bike, transit. Isochrone. area can reach point within time limit, polygon. Catchment. reverse isochrone: every block can reach place.

title: Client for the Close API (api.close.city)
text: Set output client, per call: output = "spatial" (default) returns sf object geometry applies data.frame otherwise. Block routes join census-block boundaries tigris package, downloaded cached. output = "tabular" returns data.frame every route never downloads boundaries. Reach want numbers. output = "raw" returns underlying close_reply, parsed body $data token counts alongside.

title: Client for the Close API (api.close.city)
text: Failed requests raise classed condition. Catch base close_api_error, specific one close_api_tokens_exhausted.
code: tryCatch(   close$block_summary("000000000000000"),   close_api_error = function(e) message(sprintf("%s (%d)", e$slug, e$status)) ) #> block-not-found (404)

title: Client for the Close API (api.close.city)
text: Documentation: https://henryspatialanalysis.github.io/closecity-r/ Interactive API: https://api.close.city/docs Machine-readable contract: https://api.close.city/openapi.json

title: Close API client — CloseClient
text: R6 object holds connection settings gives one method per public route. Create close_client() rather calling $new(). Results come back per output field: sf object geometry applies, data frame otherwise, close_reply output 'raw'.

title: Close API client — CloseClient
text: output (character(1)) results come back: 'spatial', 'tabular', 'raw'. Change time, override per call method's output argument.

title: Close API client — CloseClient
text: CloseClient$new() CloseClient$health() CloseClient$last_updated() CloseClient$modes() CloseClient$destination_types() CloseClient$vintage() CloseClient$places() CloseClient$block_summary() CloseClient$block_pois() CloseClient$point_summary() CloseClient$point_pois() CloseClient$pois_search() CloseClient$poi() CloseClient$poi_catchment() CloseClient$blocks_query() CloseClient$place_blocks() CloseClient$isochrone() CloseClient$isochrone_meta() CloseClient$records() CloseClient$clone()

title: Close API client — CloseClient

title: Close API client — CloseClient
text: block Origin block GEOID (give lon + lat). lon, lat Origin point, instead block. mode "walk", "bike", "transit". direction "" (blocks can reach origin) "". minutes single threshold (1 60). contours four ascending levels, instead minutes. format "geojson" (polygons) "blocks" (block list). v Optional cache-buster, echoed back. if_none_match ETag revalidate free. output Override client's output mode call.

what: Convert a Close reply to a data.frame — close_as_df
title: Convert a Close reply to a data.frame — close_as_df
text: Turns close_reply plain data.frame, one row per record. client returns "tabular" output mode; close_as_sf() adds geometry top rows. Metering envelope metadata (token counts, block_geoid, assumptions, ...) attached attributes.

title: Convert a Close reply to a data.frame — close_as_df
code: close_as_df(x, key = NULL)

title: Convert a Close reply to a data.frame — close_as_df
text: x (close_reply) reply, list shape. key (character(1), default NULL) Name record array read. Detected body omitted.

title: Convert a Close reply to a data.frame — close_as_df
text: data.frame. Summary replies gain broadcast geoid column.

title: Convert a Close reply to a data.frame — close_as_df
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ close <- close_client(output = 'raw') close_as_df(close$modes()) } # }

what: Convert a Close reply to an sf object — close_as_sf
title: Convert a Close reply to an sf object — close_as_sf
text: Detects geometry payload. POI replies ($pois_search(), $block_pois(), $point_pois(), $poi(), $places()) become points lat/lon. isochrone reply format = "geojson" becomes polygons. Block replies ($blocks_query(), $place_blocks(), $poi_catchment(), isochrone format = "blocks") carry GEOIDs, block boundaries joined block_geometry, downloaded tigris fetch = TRUE.

title: Convert a Close reply to an sf object — close_as_sf
code: close_as_sf(   x,   block_geometry = NULL,   geoid_col = "GEOID20",   crs = 4326,   fetch = FALSE )

title: Convert a Close reply to an sf object — close_as_sf
text: x (close_reply) reply, list shape. block_geometry (sf, default NULL) Block boundaries geoid_col column, joined block replies 15-digit GEOID. geoid_col (character(1), default 'GEOID20') Name GEOID column block_geometry (TIGER 2020 blocks). crs (default 4326) Coordinate reference system point polygon geometry. fetch (logical(1), default FALSE) TRUE block_geometry NULL, download needed TIGER blocks tigris (inferring state county GEOIDs).

title: Convert a Close reply to an sf object — close_as_sf
text: sf data frame. Metering envelope metadata attached attributes.

title: Convert a Close reply to an sf object — close_as_sf
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ close <- close_client(output = 'raw') close_as_sf(close$pois_search(lat = 41.82, lon = -71.41, radius_m = 1500)) } # }

what: Create a Close API client — close_client
title: Create a Close API client — close_client
text: Builds CloseClient. catalog health routes free, key optional. Every data route needs one (ck_live_ key), created https://account.close.city.

title: Create a Close API client — close_client
code: close_client(   api_key = NULL,   base_url = DEFAULT_BASE_URL,   timeout = 30,   output = "spatial" )

title: Create a Close API client — close_client
text: api_key (character(1), default NULL) API key, NULL free routes. base_url (character(1)) API base URL. timeout (numeric(1), default 30) Request timeout, seconds. output (character(1), default 'spatial') results come back: 'spatial' returns sf object geometry applies data frame otherwise; 'tabular' returns data frame every route never downloads block boundaries; 'raw' returns close_reply.

title: Create a Close API client — close_client
text: CloseClient. Make calls methods.

title: Create a Close API client — close_client
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ close <- close_client('ck_live_your_key')   # use your own key here close$block_summary('440070008001068', mode = 'walk') } # }

title: The reply object — close_reply
text: output 'raw', every method returns close_reply: list parsed body plus metering caching information named fields.

title: The reply object — close_reply
text: data parsed body (list), NULL 304. results results array list routes (else empty list). next_cursor cursor next page, NULL. tokens_charged, tokens_remaining Token counts; NULL free routes. etag response ETag, pass back if_none_match. status HTTP status code. not_modified TRUE 304 (data NULL). request_id Server request id, handy support.

title: Print a close_reply — print.close_reply
text: Print close_reply

title: Print a close_reply — print.close_reply
code: # S3 method for class 'close_reply' print(x, ...)

title: Print a close_reply — print.close_reply
text: x close_reply. ... Ignored.

title: Print a close_reply — print.close_reply
text: x, invisibly.

title: closecity 1.1.0
text: Tabular results default, across every route. New output argument replaces spatial: close_client(output = "spatial") (default) returns sf object geometry applies plain data frame catalog summary routes; output = "tabular" returns data frame every route never downloads block boundaries (cheap path want numbers); output = "raw" returns close_reply. Set client per call. Catalog routes ($modes(), $destination_types(), $vintage(), $places()) block point summaries now return data frames. $block_summary() / $point_summary() broadcast origin GEOID geoid column, $isochrone(format = "blocks") now converts . Metering envelope metadata (token counts, block_geoid, assumptions, …) attached attributes returned frame. New close_as_df() .data.frame() method close_reply, beside existing close_as_sf(). Requires R >= 4.1 (native pipe used internally).

title: closecity 1.0.0
text: First public release closecity R client Close API (api.close.city). R6 client, close_client(), one method per public route: catalog, place lookup, block point summaries POIs, POI search, detail, catchment, areal block queries, isochrones. Feature methods return sf objects default. Set spatial = FALSE work raw reply. Block boundaries joined tigris. First-class metering (tokens_charged, tokens_remaining), ETag/304 conditional requests, keyset pagination via $records(), typed RFC 9457 errors classed conditions. $places() looks city town name returns GEOID centre.
